content:
{
  "title": "Meta-Prompting: Dynamic Self-Reflective Prompt Templates for Robust LLM Defense Against Jailbreaks",
  "problem": "Current prompting strategies for LLMs are vulnerable to adversarial jailbreaks that bypass static instructions. We seek to design a prompting method that dynamically adapts to potentially adversarial user intent, reducing susceptibility to harmful outputs.",
  "motivation": "Jailbreak attacks exploit static prompt structures, and as model deployments grow, so does the sophistication of such attacks. Introducing mechanisms for LLMs to self-reflect on user intent in real-time could proactively defuse adversarial attempts before content generation, improving safety without extensive retraining.",
  "method": "We propose Meta-Prompting: augmenting standard prompts with self-reflective sub-prompts that instruct the LLM to (1) first generate an explicit natural-language summary of inferred user intent, (2) explicitly assess if this intent appears adversarial or jailbreak-seeking, and (3) only proceed to answer if the previous step is not suspicious, otherwise respond with a refusal or clarification request. The meta-prompt template is injected dynamically at inference, adapting its language and thresholds based on conversation context and user history. This approach is model-agnostic and requires no finetuning.",
  "experiment_plan": "Evaluate on JailbreakBench and custom adversarial prompt sets, comparing against static prompting and AdaShield. Metrics include jailbreak success rate, false-positive refusals, and response helpfulness on benign queries. Ablation studies will isolate the impact of intent summarization and dynamic adaptation."
}
